basic_details.job_title: OSSSC Nursing Officer Recruitment 2026 - 5989 Vacancies, Last Date 13 July 2026 | osssc.gov.in
basic_details.short_information: The Odisha Sub-ordinate Staff Selection Commission (OSSSC) is recruiting 5989 District Cadre Group-C Nursing Officers across all 30 districts of Odisha under the Health & Family Welfare Department. Online registration opens on 13 June 2026 and the last date to apply is 13 July 2026. The examination fee is ₹500 for all categories except SC, ST, and PwDs. Apply exclusively through www.osssc.gov.in.

application_fee.details: The examination fee is straightforward, with a full exemption for reserved categories. Candidates who pay the fee and actually appear in the examination will receive a refund after the examination is conducted and attendance records are verified. | The fee of ₹500 must be paid positively using Net Banking, Credit Card, Debit Card, or UPI through the Payment Gateway available during online application submission. If the Confirmation Page is not generated after payment, the transaction will be cancelled and the refund will be credited within 48 hours. If no Confirmation Page is generated, the applicant must make another payment. | The ₹500 fee will be refunded to candidates who actually appear in the examination. The refund will be processed within a reasonable period after the examination and verification of attendance records. No refund is admissible to applicants who fail to appear in the examination for any reason. The refund will be credited to the same bank account or payment method used during registration - candidates are advised to use their own account for smooth refund processing.

age_limit.details: Age relaxation: | Category Relaxation SC / ST / SEBC / Women 5 years (upper age limit becomes 47 years) Persons with Disabilities (PwDs) 10 years (upper age limit becomes 52 years) PwDs under SC/ST/SEBC Cumulative 10 years over and above SC/ST/SEBC relaxation Ex-Servicemen Relaxable by the total period of defence service rendered Contractual Nursing Officer (OSH&FW Society/Scheme and ASHAs) Upper age limit is 45 years | Candidates falling under more than one relaxation category are eligible for only the single most beneficial relaxation, except PwDs under SC/ST/SEBC who get cumulative relaxation as specified. Ex-Servicemen who have already secured regular State Government employment and availed the Ex-Servicemen benefit once are not eligible for reservation under Ex-Servicemen category for subsequent recruitment.

qualification.details: Age relaxation: | Candidates falling under more than one relaxation category are eligible for only the single most beneficial relaxation, except PwDs under SC/ST/SEBC who get cumulative relaxation as specified. Ex-Servicemen who have already secured regular State Government employment and availed the Ex-Servicemen benefit once are not eligible for reservation under Ex-Servicemen category for subsequent recruitment. | Candidates must satisfy all three of the following: | 1. Academic Qualification: Must have passed 10+2 in the 10+2 system or its equivalent. | 2. Professional Qualification: Must hold a Diploma in GNM (General Nursing & Midwifery) or BSc Nursing from any Medical College and Hospitals of the State or any other institution duly recognised and approved by the Indian Nursing Council. | 3. Registration: Must have registered their name in the Nursing Council in the State of Odisha and must possess a valid Registration or Renewal Certificate as on the date of submission of the online application form. This certificate must be uploaded online at the time of application. | 4. Odia Language: Candidates must be able to speak, read, and write Odia, and must have satisfied one of the following: passed the Middle School Examination with Odia as a language subject; passed Matriculation or equivalent with Odia as the medium of instruction in non-language subjects; passed Odia as a language subject in the final examination of Class-VII or above from a School recognised by the Government of Odisha or Central Government; or passed a test in Odia in M.E. School Standard conducted by the School & Mass Education Department. | Candidates must be Indian citizens and of good character. Married candidates must not have more than one spouse living. Sportsperson category claimants must possess a Sportsperson ID card issued by the Sports & Youth Services Department, Government of Odisha and upload the scanned copy of the original (not photocopy). Candidates debarred from any OSSSC recruitment for a specified period are not eligible during that period.

salary_details.details: The Nursing Officer post carries a clearly defined pay scale under the Odisha Revised Scale of Pay (ORSP) Rules 2017. This is a permanent Group-C post in the district cadre. | The starting basic pay for a Nursing Officer is ₹29,200 per month at Pay Matrix Level-8, Cell-01 as per ORSP Rules 2017, with applicable Dearness Allowance and other allowances as per Government rules. Candidates selected may also be deployed as Community Health Officer (CHO) by the appointing authorities.

selection_process: The selection is based on a single Written Test followed by Document Verification. No interview is conducted. The selection process has three stages: Written Test
and Document Verification followed by a Provisional Select List.
The Written Test consists of 100 questions for 100 marks to be completed in 2 hours. The exact mode (OMR or CBRE) will be notified separately. The test paper is divided as follows:
Negative Marking: For every wrong answer
0.25 marks will be deducted as penalty. Each question has four options. If a candidate marks more than one option
it is treated as a wrong answer. Unanswered questions carry no penalty.
Minimum Qualifying Marks: Candidates belonging to ST
and Sportsperson categories must score a minimum of 30% (normalized score). All other candidates must score a minimum of 35% (normalized score).

Tie-breaking sequence (applied in order if two candidates score identically): (a) fewer wrong attempts; (b) higher percentage of marks in Diploma in GNM or BSc Nursing; (c) age seniority (older candidate ranked higher); (d) higher marks in +2 examination; (e) Commission's discretion.
the test will be conducted in multiple shifts on different dates and a normalization formula will be applied to convert raw scores to normalized scores.

a Provisional Master Merit List will be prepared in order of marks/normalized scores. Candidates who meet the minimum qualifying marks will be shortlisted for Document Verification at not less than 1.25 times the number of advertised vacancies

Shortlisted candidates will be called to appear before the District Level Board in the office of the Collector and District Magistrate of each district. Original documents will be verified by the Chief District Medical & Public Health Officers. Failure to attend Document Verification will result in rejection of candidature. Candidature remains provisional until validated by the appointing authority.
A Provisional Select List equal to the number of advertised vacancies will be drawn from the DV screening list
and the candidate's district preference as exercised during application or physical counselling.
call shortlisted candidates for physical counselling for district allocation
based on merit-cum-choice as per actual vacancies available.

Written Test Syllabus (as per Annexure-II): Anatomy & Physiology; Microbiology; Psychology; Sociology; Nursing Foundation; First Aid; Community Health Nursing; Nutrition; Medical Surgical Nursing/Adult Health Nursing (as per revised BSc Nursing syllabus 2021); Mental Health Nursing; Child Health Nursing; Midwifery & Gynaecological Nursing; Nursing Education; Research and Statistics; Professionalism
Professional values & ethics; Nursing Administration & Management.
how_to_apply: The application process has two mandatory steps - Registration and Application Submission - which are separate operations but corollary to each other. Completing registration without submitting the application form means the application is incomplete and will be rejected.
Visit the OSSSC official website at www.osssc.gov.in.

On the Home page, click "Apply Online" if you are a New User (not registered earlier for any OSSSC recruitment). If you are a Registered User (already registered for any earlier OSSSC post and have a User ID), click the "Re-registration" option under the Applicant Menu to re-register for this post.
Read the instructions carefully by clicking "How do I register/re-register/apply" on the Home page before filling the Registration Form.

Complete the Registration Form first. Keep the following ready before starting: scanned signature in jpg/jpeg/png format between 20 KB and 50 KB; scanned colour passport-size photograph in jpg/jpeg/png format between 20 KB and 100 KB; valid personal email ID and mobile/cell phone number (to be kept active throughout the recruitment process).
After successful registration, log in and fill the Online Application Form with all required details and upload documents as per the guidance of the online system.
Indicate district preferences (up to the districts available) in order of priority.
Pay the examination fee of ₹500 (if applicable) using Net Banking/Credit Card/Debit Card/UPI through the Payment Gateway. After successful payment, print the Confirmation Page.
Submit the online application. Incomplete applications will be summarily rejected. No Admission Letter will be issued to candidates whose application is found incomplete.
Keep the User ID, Password, and registration number safely - these will be required for downloading the Admission Letter and future communications from OSSSC.

content_sections.heading: OSSSC Nursing Officer Recruitment 2026 - Quick Summary
content_sections.text: The recruitment is being conducted under the Odisha Nursing Service (Method of Recruitment and Conditions of Service) Rules, 2019 and its subsequent amendment rules up to date. Candidates selected may also be deployed as Community Health Officer (CHO) by the respective Appointing Authorities.

content_sections.heading: OSSSC Nursing Officer Recruitment 2026 - Important Dates
content_sections.text: The application window is open for exactly one month. Candidates are strongly advised not to wait until the last date, as the online system is automatically disabled at midnight on the closing date and no application will be accepted thereafter. Registration and application submission are two separate steps. Completing registration alone does not mean the application has been filed. Candidates must log in after registration and submit the full online application form before 13 July 2026 .

content_sections.heading: Vacancy Breakdown - District-wise and Category-wise
content_sections.text: The 5989 vacancies are spread across all 30 districts of Odisha. These are district cadre posts, meaning selected candidates will be posted and transferred only within the district they are allocated. Candidates must indicate their district preferences in the online application form in order of priority. Category-wise Total Vacancies: Special Category Vacancies (included within district totals above): Top 5 districts by total vacancies: The number of posts to be filled may change at the Government's discretion without prior notice. Reservations for all categories will be applied in accordance with relevant Odisha Government Acts, Rules, and Notifications in force.

tables.caption: Special Category Vacancies (included within district totals above):

content_sections.heading: OSSSC Nursing Officer Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: Age relaxation: Candidates falling under more than one relaxation category are eligible for only the single most beneficial relaxation, except PwDs under SC/ST/SEBC who get cumulative relaxation as specified. Ex-Servicemen who have already secured regular State Government employment and availed the Ex-Servicemen benefit once are not eligible for reservation under Ex-Servicemen category for subsequent recruitment. Candidates must satisfy all three of the following: 1. Academic Qualification: Must have passed 10+2 in the 10+2 system or its equivalent. 2. Professional Qualification: Must hold a Diploma in GNM (General Nursing & Midwifery) or BSc Nursing from any Medical College and Hospitals of the State or any other institution duly recognised and approved by the Indian Nursing Council . 3. Registration: Must have registered their name in the Nursing Council in the State of Odisha and must possess a valid Registration or Renewal Certificate as on the date of submission of the online application form . This certificate must be uploaded online at the time of application. 4. Odia Language: Candidates must be able to speak, read, and write Odia, and must have satisfied one of the following: passed the Middle School Examination with Odia as a language subject; passed Matriculation or equivalent with Odia as the medium of instruction in non-language subjects; passed Odia as a language subject in the final examination of Class-VII or above from a School recognised by the Government of Odisha or Central Government; or passed a test in Odia in M.E. School Standard conducted by the School & Mass Education Department. Candidates must be Indian citizens and of good character. Married candidates must not have more than one spouse living. Sportsperson category claimants must possess a Sportsperson ID card issued by the Sports & Youth Services Department, Government of Odisha and upload the scanned copy of the original (not photocopy). Candidates debarred from any OSSSC recruitment for a specified period are not eligible during that period.

rows.relaxation: Cumulative 10 years over and above SC/ST/SEBC relaxation
Relaxable by the total period of defence service rendered

rows.category: Contractual Nursing Officer (OSH&FW Society/Scheme and ASHAs)

content_sections.heading: OSSSC Nursing Officer Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee
content_sections.text: The examination fee is straightforward, with a full exemption for reserved categories. Candidates who pay the fee and actually appear in the examination will receive a refund after the examination is conducted and attendance records are verified. The fee of ₹500 must be paid positively using Net Banking, Credit Card, Debit Card, or UPI through the Payment Gateway available during online application submission. If the Confirmation Page is not generated after payment, the transaction will be cancelled and the refund will be credited within 48 hours . If no Confirmation Page is generated, the applicant must make another payment. The ₹500 fee will be refunded to candidates who actually appear in the examination. The refund will be processed within a reasonable period after the examination and verification of attendance records. No refund is admissible to applicants who fail to appear in the examination for any reason. The refund will be credited to the same bank account or payment method used during registration - candidates are advised to use their own account for smooth refund processing.

rows.category: All candidates other than SC, ST, and PwDs

content_sections.heading: OSSSC Nursing Officer Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process
content_sections.text: The selection is based on a single Written Test followed by Document Verification. No interview is conducted. The selection process has three stages: Written Test, Provisional Master Merit List, and Document Verification followed by a Provisional Select List. Stage 1 - Written Test (100 marks, MCQ, OMR/CBRE Mode) The Written Test consists of 100 questions for 100 marks to be completed in 2 hours . The exact mode (OMR or CBRE) will be notified separately. The test paper is divided as follows: Negative Marking: For every wrong answer, 0.25 marks will be deducted as penalty. Each question has four options. If a candidate marks more than one option, it is treated as a wrong answer. Unanswered questions carry no penalty. Minimum Qualifying Marks: Candidates belonging to ST, SC, PwDs, Ex-Servicemen, and Sportsperson categories must score a minimum of 30% (normalized score). All other candidates must score a minimum of 35% (normalized score). Tie-breaking sequence (applied in order if two candidates score identically): (a) fewer wrong attempts; (b) higher percentage of marks in Diploma in GNM or BSc Nursing; (c) age seniority (older candidate ranked higher); (d) higher marks in +2 examination; (e) Commission's discretion. In CBRE mode, the test will be conducted in multiple shifts on different dates and a normalization formula will be applied to convert raw scores to normalized scores. Stage 2 - Provisional Master Merit List After the Written Test, a Provisional Master Merit List will be prepared in order of marks/normalized scores. Candidates who meet the minimum qualifying marks will be shortlisted for Document Verification at not less than 1.25 times the number of advertised vacancies, category/special category wise. Stage 3 - Document Verification (DV) Shortlisted candidates will be called to appear before the District Level Board in the office of the Collector and District Magistrate of each district. Original documents will be verified by the Chief District Medical & Public Health Officers. Failure to attend Document Verification will result in rejection of candidature. Candidature remains provisional until validated by the appointing authority. Stage 4 - Provisional Select List A Provisional Select List equal to the number of advertised vacancies will be drawn from the DV screening list, based strictly on merit, availability, and the candidate's district preference as exercised during application or physical counselling. Physical Counselling (Optional): The Commission may, at its discretion, call shortlisted candidates for physical counselling for district allocation, based on merit-cum-choice as per actual vacancies available. Written Test Syllabus (as per Annexure-II): Anatomy & Physiology; Microbiology; Psychology; Sociology; Nursing Foundation; First Aid; Community Health Nursing; Nutrition; Medical Surgical Nursing/Adult Health Nursing (as per revised BSc Nursing syllabus 2021); Mental Health Nursing; Child Health Nursing; Midwifery & Gynaec

content_sections.heading: OSSSC Nursing Officer Recruitment 2026 - How to Apply
content_sections.text: The application process has two mandatory steps - Registration and Application Submission - which are separate operations but corollary to each other. Completing registration without submitting the application form means the application is incomplete and will be rejected. Visit the OSSSC official website at www.osssc.gov.in . On the Home page, click "Apply Online" if you are a New User (not registered earlier for any OSSSC recruitment). If you are a Registered User (already registered for any earlier OSSSC post and have a User ID), click the "Re-registration" option under the Applicant Menu to re-register for this post. Read the instructions carefully by clicking "How do I register/re-register/apply" on the Home page before filling the Registration Form. Complete the Registration Form first. Keep the following ready before starting: scanned signature in jpg/jpeg/png format between 20 KB and 50 KB ; scanned colour passport-size photograph in jpg/jpeg/png format between 20 KB and 100 KB ; valid personal email ID and mobile/cell phone number (to be kept active throughout the recruitment process). After successful registration, log in and fill the Online Application Form with all required details and upload documents as per the guidance of the online system. Indicate district preferences (up to the districts available) in order of priority. Pay the examination fee of ₹500 (if applicable) using Net Banking/Credit Card/Debit Card/UPI through the Payment Gateway. After successful payment, print the Confirmation Page . Submit the online application. Incomplete applications will be summarily rejected. No Admission Letter will be issued to candidates whose application is found incomplete. Keep the User ID, Password, and registration number safely - these will be required for downloading the Admission Letter and future communications from OSSSC.

content_sections.heading: OSSSC Nursing Officer Recruitment 2026 - Salary / Pay Scale
content_sections.text: The Nursing Officer post carries a clearly defined pay scale under the Odisha Revised Scale of Pay (ORSP) Rules 2017. This is a permanent Group-C post in the district cadre. The starting basic pay for a Nursing Officer is ₹29,200 per month at Pay Matrix Level-8, Cell-01 as per ORSP Rules 2017, with applicable Dearness Allowance and other allowances as per Government rules. Candidates selected may also be deployed as Community Health Officer (CHO) by the appointing authorities.

content_sections.heading: Documents Required for Document Verification
content_sections.text: Candidates who qualify the Written Test and are shortlisted for Document Verification must produce all original certificates. The following documents are required: Certificate of Registration in the Nursing Council of the State of Odisha (valid as on the date of online application submission). HSC examination certificate or equivalent, 10+2 pass certificate or equivalent, Diploma in GNM or BSc Nursing certificate and mark sheets. Certificate of passing Odia as a language subject (Class-VII or above from a recognised school/institution) or certificate of passing a test in Odia in M.E. School Standard. Caste certificate (SC/ST/SEBC), or UDID card/Disability Certificate showing permanent disability issued by the Competent Authority (for PwDs). Discharge Certificate/Identity Card and documents in support of defence service rendered, along with an undertaking of non-availing of re-employment under State Government (for Ex-Servicemen). Certificate/documents of contractual work experience under OSH&FW Society/Scheme or as ASHA (for contractual Nursing Officers claiming age relaxation). Sports ID card issued by the Sports & Youth Services Department, Government of Odisha (for Sportsperson category claimants). PwD candidates must possess a Permanent Disability Certificate showing not less than 40% disability and must upload the scanned copy of the original (not photocopy) UDID Card/Disability Certificate. Applicants with temporary disability are not eligible and such applications will be summarily rejected.

content_sections.heading: Troubleshooting and Common Mistakes
content_sections.text: Applying online for a large-scale recruitment like this involves several steps where errors are common. Following these general practices will help avoid rejection or delays. Photo and Signature Errors: The photograph must be a recent colour passport-size image in jpg/jpeg/png format, scanned between 20 KB and 100 KB . The signature must be a full specimen signature in jpg/jpeg/png format between 20 KB and 50 KB . Files outside these size ranges will not be accepted by the portal. Both must be clearly identifiable; blurred or unclear images will lead to rejection of the registration/application. Two-Step Confusion: A very common mistake is treating Registration as the final step. Registration and Application Submission are separate operations. Always log back in after registering to fill and submit the Online Application Form - only then is your application considered filed. Payment and Confirmation Page: If the Confirmation Page does not appear after fee payment, do not close the browser immediately. If the page is genuinely not generated, the transaction will be cancelled and refunded within 48 hours - you must then make a fresh payment. Do not make multiple payments simultaneously. Use your own bank account or UPI ID to ensure the refund reaches you without complications. Email ID and Mobile Number: Use a valid personal email ID and mobile number that will remain active throughout the entire recruitment process, including result and admission letter downloads. Do not share your User ID and Password with anyone, and do not change your registered email or mobile number during the process. Multiple Applications: Submitting more than one Application Form for the same post will lead to cancellation of results and disqualification. Under no circumstances should a candidate fill more than one form. Last-Day Rush: The OSSSC system will be automatically disabled after the deadline. Submitting close to 13 July 2026 risks server overload and session timeouts. Apply and submit well in advance. Contractual Nursing Officers claiming age relaxation: Must upload appointment letters from their employers with self-attestation online, and must keep original appointment letters ready for verification after the Written Test.

content_sections.heading: OSSSC Nursing Officer Recruitment 2026 - Important Links

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for OSSSC Nursing Officer 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application for OSSSC Nursing Officer 2026 is 13 July 2026. The online system will be automatically disabled after this date and no application will be accepted thereafter.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are there in OSSSC Nursing Officer 2026?
faq.answer: The OSSSC has notified 5989 vacancies for the post of Nursing Officer across all 30 districts of Odisha under the Health & Family Welfare Department.

faq.question: Q3. What is the educational qualification required for OSSSC Nursing Officer 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must have passed 10+2, hold a Diploma in GNM or BSc Nursing from a recognised institution approved by the Indian Nursing Council, and possess a valid Registration or Renewal Certificate from the Nursing Council of Odisha as on the date of submission of the online application form.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for OSSSC Nursing Officer 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must be between 21 and 42 years of age as on the date of advertisement (12 June 2026). SC/ST/SEBC/Women candidates get a 5-year relaxation (upper limit becomes 47 years), PwD candidates get a 10-year relaxation (upper limit becomes 52 years), and contractual Nursing Officers under OSH&FW Society/Scheme and ASHAs get an upper limit of 45 years.

faq.question: Q5. What is the application fee for OSSSC Nursing Officer 2026?
faq.answer: The examination fee is ₹500 for all candidates except those belonging to SC, ST, and Persons with Disabilities (PwDs) categories, who are exempted from paying any fee. The fee can be paid via Net Banking, Credit Card, Debit Card, or UPI.

faq.question: Q6. What is the pay scale for OSSSC Nursing Officer 2026?
faq.answer: The Nursing Officer post carries a pay scale of ₹29,200–92,300 at Pay Matrix Level-8, Cell-01 as per Odisha Revised Scale of Pay (ORSP) Rules 2017. The starting basic pay is ₹29,200 per month.

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process for OSSSC Nursing Officer 2026?
faq.answer: Selection is based on a Written Test of 100 marks (MCQ type, OMR/CBRE mode, 2 hours duration) followed by Document Verification and preparation of a Provisional Select List. There is no interview. Minimum qualifying marks are 30% for ST/SC/PwDs/Ex-Servicemen/Sportsperson and 35% for all others.

faq.question: Q8. What is the exam pattern for OSSSC Nursing Officer Written Test?
faq.answer: The Written Test has 100 questions for 100 marks in 2 hours. The paper includes 60 questions from nursing syllabus subjects, 25 questions on practical skills, 10 questions on Arithmetic (High School level), and 5 questions on English (High School level). There is negative marking of 0.25 marks per wrong answer.

faq.question: What is the last date to apply for OSSSC Nursing Officer 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application for OSSSC Nursing Officer 2026 is 13 July 2026. The online system will be automatically disabled after this date and no application will be accepted thereafter.
